response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 30+ years of broad technical experience and a genuine open-source track record, but the role has two hard requirements he does not meet: German proficiency at C1 minimum (he lists 'Basic') and demonstrated leadership over teams and client engagements. His career reads as a deep individual-contributor trajectory with no management titles, no explicit client advisory work, and no mention of UML or Python — both listed as desired skills. The language gap alone would make client-facing consulting in a German-market firm untenable.
strengths: Exceptionally broad technical depth spanning 30+ years across web, embedded, mobile, and infrastructure domains
Strong open-source orientation with shipped projects (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, vibe.dav) and a FOSS4G conference talk
Linux and systems-level experience (DLang, C/C++, microservices, high-throughput I/O) aligns with the open-source/Linux emphasis
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — role requires minimum C1 for client communication in a German IT consulting context; this is a hard disqualifier
No evidence of team leadership, people management, or client-facing advisory responsibilities across any role
Neither UML nor Python appear anywhere in the resume, yet both are explicitly listed as required/desired skills for the position
